failed_attempts:
intro h
  cases h
  <;> simp_all [Option.some_ne_none]
  <;> aesop
  <;> simp_all [Option.some_ne_none]
  <;> aesop
  <;> simp_all [Option.some_ne_none]
  <;> aesop

intro h
  rcases h with ⟨⟩
  <;> simp_all [IsSome]
  <;> aesop

have h_main : ∀ (o : Option α), IsSome o → o ≠ none := by
    intro o h
    cases o with
    | some a => intro hnone; simp_all [Option.IsSome]
    | none => simp_all [Option.IsSome]
  
  intro h_is_some
  have h₁ : (none : Option α) ≠ none := h_main none h_is_some
  contradiction

intro h
  cases h
  <;> simp_all [Option.isSome]
  <;> aesop

intro h
  cases h
  <;> simp_all [Option.IsSome]
  <;> aesop

intro h
  -- We use the fact that the only way to construct an IsSome is to have a 'some' value
  -- Since none is not a some value, we derive a contradiction
  cases h
  <;> simp_all
  <;> aesop
successful_attempts:
intro h
  cases h
  <;> simp_all
  <;> contradiction

intro h
  cases h
  <;> simp_all (config := {decide := true})

intro h
  cases h
  <;> simp_all [Option.some_eq_coe]
  <;> contradiction

intro h
  cases h <;> simp_all [Option.IsSome]
  <;> contradiction

intro h
  -- Assume for contradiction that none is a some
  cases h <;> simp_all [Option.IsSome]
  <;> contradiction

intro h
  cases h
  <;> simp_all (config := {decide := true})

have h₁ : ∀ (h : IsSome (α := α) none), False := by
    intro h
    cases h
  have h₂ : ¬IsSome (α := α) none := by
    intro h
    have h₃ : False := h₁ h
    exact h₃
  exact h₂

intro h
  cases h
  <;> simp_all
  <;> try contradiction
  <;> try simp_all
